more: n. f. Sown lands; corn-fields; crops. 1. Khetī khasam setī . Prov. To till the land you need the owner's hand. (If you want a thing done do it yourself). 2. Bādhe pūt pitā ke dharmā, khetī upjaë apne karmā . Prov. The son thrives on his father's piety, the field is as your assiduity. 3. Charkhā bechūṅ, ponī bechāṅ, mārū jī ko le dūn bail, hamāro saiyāṅ khetī kar le! Tīn bīgah ke terah dīne, ḍanḍ meṅ de dīyo bail, hamāro saīyāṅ aur bo le! Rus. Wom. song. My spinning-wheel I'll sell, my cotton I will sell, an ox I'll for my husband buy, my husband, plough away! For bīgahs three thirteen given up, and eke an ox as fine—put down the seed, my spouse! 4. Be-khasam ke khetī, be-mard ke joë, Be-mātā ke larikā jāme, tīnoṅ rahe roë . Bhoj. A field without an owner, as a wife without her mate, And eke a child that's motherless, these three bemoan their fate. khetī bāṛī, khetī kyārī, khetī , n. f. k…

def: s.f. Parched grain or rice (inflated or puffed out so as to appear like froth; — syn. khoʼī, lāwā).
more: n. f. Pl. khīleṅ . 1. Parched grain or rice. 2. A bit; particle. khīl batāse . Parched rice and batāse , 2, q. v. Jis din khīl batāsoṅ kā meṅh barsā thā . ☞ It is related of a noted Sheikhchillī (fool) that he once brought home some stolen property which his mother hid away. She, however, fearing lest his stupidity should lead to discovery, contrived to let fall unseen a shower of khīl batāshās (parched rice and sugar lumps) which the fool was made to believe had dropped from the sky. In due course enquiries were set on foot and the fool confessed to the theft. But his mother pleaded that he was an idiot, and by way of proof suggested the question 'when was the theft committed?' The fool promptly replied 'Dont I know, it was the day it rained khīl batasās .' khīl khīl k. , v. a. To break in pieces. khīl khīl h . v. n. To be broken to pieces.
s. f. Parched grain inflated or puffed out by heat so as to appear like froth.

def: v.n. To open, expand (as a flower), to blow, bloom, flower; to open, crack, burst, swell (as a wall, or plaster, or parched grain, &c.; cf. khīl); to break out, show itself or its effects (as intoxicating liquor, &c.); — to be set off (by), to show to advantage (on, -par), to look well or becoming (as a dress or a person, or one colour upon another); — to expand or swell (with pleasure), to be exhilarated, be delighted; to rejoice, laugh
more: v. n. S. कृ 1. To blow ( phūlnā , 3,); bloom. Jab merī galī tū āëgo, merī khil jāëgī sab kalī kalī, Bhauṅrā ban-ke āë jā tū merī galī! Farrukh. Wom. When in my lane you come, like a flower bud I will bloom, Oh be a bee and make this lane your chosen home! 2. To open; swell; burst. 3. To be kept separate or apart. Naë chāṅval kam khilte haiṅ . 4. To affect one's head or go to the head. Ab jā-ke nashah khilā hai . 5. To crack; to be broken, fractured, split. 1. Sārī divār khil gaī . 2. Yeh makaī khūb khilī! 6. To look well; suit; become. Harī ṭopī tumhāre sir pe nahīṅ khilti . 7. To swell with pleasure; to be exhilirated ( phūlnā , 4). Āj to khūb khil rahe ho! khil'nī , adj. Swollen; bursting. Khilnī juār. khil'vāṅ , adj. Separated; disjoined; apart. Khilvāṅ khichṛī .
v. n. 1. To blow (as a flower), to flower. 2. To be delighted, to laugh.

def: adj. Hoggish, swinish; — s.f. A hog-sty.
s.f. Rice-milk, a dish made of rice and milk (rice, parboiled in water, is again boiled in milk, with the addition of sugar, and sometimes spices and kernels)
more: n. f. S. क्षीर, Pr. chhīrām ; Pālī, khīram milk. A mess of rice and milk. 1. Ran kī sobhā sūrmāṅ, ghar kī sobhā bīr, Raj kī sobhā chāṅdnī, bhojan sobhā khīr . The brave the battlefield, the house the wife doth grace, The night the moon adorns, in feasts khīr holds chief place. 2. Maiṅ ne kyā uskī khīr khāī hai ? Prov. saying. Have I eaten any of his rice and milk. (Am I under any obligation to him?). khīr-chaṭāī , n. f. The ceremony of giving a child rice-milk for the first time as soon as he is weaned. khīr kā dalyā ho jānā , v. n. lit . to be turned from khīr to coarse meal. 1. To suffer a reverse of fortune. 2. To be destroyed or marred (one's aims). Nazīr! yār kī ham ne jo kal ziyāfat kī, Pakāyā qarz maṅgā-kar pulāo aur qalyā, So yār āp na āyā, raqīb ko bhejā, Hazār haif! ham aise nasīb ke balyā! Idhar to qarz huā, aur udhar na āyā yār, Pakāī khīr thī, qismat se ho gayā dalyā . Nazīr…
s. f. Rice-milk. (Rice, parboiled in water, is again boiled in milk, with the addition of sugar, spices, and kernels: Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: s.f. Giving a child khīr to taste for the first time, weaning (this is done when the child is about six or seven months old); the ceremonies and festivites observed on such an occasion
'To be changed from khīr to coarse meal': to suffer a reverse of fortune; to be ruined or marred (a hope, or aim).

v.t. To move, move away, remove; to draw back or away; to shove off or away, to drive away, to put to flight; to put out of place, to displace; — to put off, to postpone; — to push (towards), to slip (into the hands of); — to decamp with, to carry off, to steal
more: v. a. 1. To move; shove ( sarkānā 1). 2. To push towards one; slip into one's hand ( sarkānā , 4). 3. To steal ( alag k . 12—16).
subs: To make off with, to carry off, to steal.

def: s.m. One who slinks away, &c.; — one who has taken leave of his senses.
adj. (f. -ī), Slippery; smooth.
Slipping, sliding; slipperiness.
v.t. To cause to slip, &c.
s.m. Causing to slip; — slipping; slipperiness.

ety: khīs˚ = S. क्लिश्य(ते), pass. of rt. क्लिश
def: v.n. To grin, to gnash or show the teeth; to be mortified.

ety: for A. خسة, inf. n. of خسّ 'to be mean or sordid,' &c.
def: s.f. Meanness, baseness, vileness; sordidness, stinginess, parsimony, avarice, penuriousness.
more: n. f. خس was mean. Stinginess (بخل).
s. f. 1. Stinginess, parsimony, avarice, penuriousness. 2. Meanness.

def: s.f. = ḵẖishtak, q.v. s.f. A small brick; — a square piece of cloth put under the arm-pit of a shirt, &c., a gusset; cod-piece; — drawers, trousers.
s.f. (Dakkhinī), Dirt, filth, impurity, &c. ( = kiṭṭ, q.v.). s.m. A kind of sour dish.
more: s. f. (dakh.) Filth, dirt, mud. کهٿ جمنا khiṭ jamnā (i. q. ميل جمنا mail jamnā ) Filth to be collected (?).

ety: inf. n. of iii of خطب 'to recite'
def: s.m. Conversation, discourse, speech, address, harangue; — a title
more: n. m. خطب he conversed. A title; honorific title Inko susrāl se 'munavvar bahū' kā khitāb milā hai . At her father-in-law's house she got the title of 'the bright daughter-in-law.' khitāb k .; Ped. for mukhātib h. , v. a. To address; accost; speak to. khitāb-o-itāb , n. m. Angry words; rebukes. Bhalā burā mūjhe ghar meṅ jo chāhnā kahnā; Nahīṅ haiṅ khūb khitāb-o-itāb raste meṅ . Wom. Hijāb.
(from خطب) s. m. 1. A title. 2. Conversation, speech, address. خطاب کرنا ḵẖitāb karnā, To address, to accost.
subs: s.m. Angry or reproachful speech, rebukes, censures.

def: s.f. (Dakkhinī) Giggling, titter; sound of laughing (cf. khilkhil).
more: s. f. (dakh.) Titter, sound of laughing.

ety: for A. خطة ḵẖit̤t̤at, fem.; v.n. fr. خطّ; see ḵẖat̤t̤
def: s.m. A piece of ground (marked out), a plot of land; district, territory, region, country.
more: s. m. A region, country, territory, district.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خِطَّه (xitta, “territory; ground; country”), from Arabic خِطَّة (ḵiṭṭa).

def: adj. (Dakkhinī) Filthy, soiled, polluted, impure (syn. mailā).
n. m. A rower; boatman; waterman.
s.m. A parterre, a flower-bed.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خِیَابَان (xiyābān).

ety: khiyā˚ = S. क्षय+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.n. To be worn, to be abraded; — v.t. To rub away, abrade; to rub down.
more: v. n. S. क्षय To be worn or abraded.
v. n. To be worn, to be abraded.

ety: khiyā˚ = khiyāw˚ = Prk. खयाव(इ) = S. खाद (contrac. to खद, rt. खाद्)+अपय(ति)
def: v.t. (dialec.) caus. of khānā, and = khilānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to eat; to give food to, to feed; to entertain; to cause to take (as medicine, or the air, or an oath, &c.); to give, to administer (poison, &c.); to cause to suffer or undergo (as a beating, &c.)
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Who feeds; — feeder, supporter, sustainer
s.f. A woman who supports her lover.

ety: for A. خيانة, inf. n. of خان, for خون 'to be unfaithful, or perfidious,' &c.
def: s.f. Unfaithfulness (to one's confidence, or to a trust); perfidy, treachery, treason; breach of trust, misappropriation; embezzlement; dishonesty, knavery
more: n. f. خون was unfaithful. A breach of trust; dishonesty ( khurd burd , 2.). /?/khayānat k. , v. a. To embezzle; misappropriate. khayānat-i-mujrimāna , Criminal breach of trust.
s. f. Perfidy, treachery, treason, knavery, embezzlement.
subs: (-meṅ), To be unfaithful to a trust; to be guilty of breach of trust, to misappropriate, to embezzle; to practise perfidy or treachery.

ety: subst. fr. iḵẖtiyār, inf. n. of viii of خير 'to be good,' &c.
def: s.m. Election, choice, option.
more: (from خير) s. m. 1. Choice, election. 2. A cucumber.

ety: the A. being der. fr. P. ḵẖiyār = H. ḵẖīrā, fr. S. क्षीर
def: s.m. A cucumber, Cucumis sativus
adj. (Dakh.) Sly, artful, deceitful.
more: adj. (dakh.) Sly, artful. See عياره aﻌiyāra.

def: s.m. (obl. dual of ḵẖiyār, q.v.), The two kinds of cucumbers, i.e. the cucumber and the musk-melon; the seeds of the cucumber and musk-melon (used medicinally).
more: n. m. Cucumber and musk-melon seeds, used medicinally.

def: s.f. Scurfiness; scurf, dandriff.

ety: khiyā˚ = khiyāw˚ = Prk. खयाव(इ) = S. खाद (contrac. to खद, rt. खाद्)+अपय(ति)
def: v.t. (dialec.) caus. of khānā, and = khilānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to eat; to give food to, to feed; to entertain; to cause to take (as medicine, or the air, or an oath, &c.); to give, to administer (poison, &c.); to cause to suffer or undergo (as a beating, &c.)
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Who feeds; — feeder, supporter, sustainer
s.f. A woman who supports her lover.

ety: inf. n. of خضب 'to colour or tinge'
def: s.m. Tinging or dyeing the nails and hair (especially the beard), hair-dye, hair-tincture (usually of a red or dark blue colour)
more: n. m. خضاب tinging. A hair dye; dying the hair, the nails and hair, and especially the beard; hair tincture. khizāb-i-āhanī phernā , v. n. To apply a razor to one's chin; to have a clean shave. khizāb laganā , v. n. To apply hair dye.
(from خضب) s. m. 1. Tincture. 2. Tinging the nails and hair, but particularly the beard. خضاب آهني کرنا ḵẖiẓāb-i-āhanī karnā, To shave the beard.
subs: To apply a razor (to the chin, &c.), to shave (the beard)
(-meṅ), To apply hair-dye, to dye the hair black.

def: s.f. Autumn, the fall of the leaf; — decay, old age
more: n. f. 1. The fall of the leaf. 2. Autumn, 3. Decay; old age.
s. f. Autumn, the fall of the leaf. خزان ديده ḵẖizān-dīda, Having seen or experienced autumn.
subs: adj. Having seen or experienced autumn.

ety: for A. خزانة ḵẖizānat, fem.; v.n. fr. خزن 'to stow, to guard,' &c.
def: s.m. A repository, magazine, store-room, granary; a treasury; — treasure, revenue, finances; — chamber (of a gun)
more: n. m. خزن laying up. 1. ( khazāna-i-āmirā, yā sarkārī ) A treasury; Government treasury; exchequer; the public revenue; finances. 2. A store; magazine. 3. A repository; reservoir. 4. The chamber of a gun or musket. khazānah kā sandūq , n. m. The till; the treasure chest.

(improper. ḵẖazāna, from خزن) s. m. 1. A treasury, magazine, granary, repository, reservoir. 2. Treasure. 3. The chamber of a gun. خزانه دار ḵẖazāna-dār, A musket with a chamber. خزانهٴعامره ḵẖazānaʼe āﻌmira, s. m. The royal treasury. (Plur. خزاين ḵẖazāʼịn ).
subs: adj. & s.m. Having a chamber (a gun); — a gun or musket with a chamber
s.m. The royal treasury, the Government treasury
wety: First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi خزانہ (xizāna), borrowed from Classical Persian خِزَانَه (xizāna), from Arabic خِزَانَة (ḵizāna).

def: adj. Autumnal; fading.
prop. n.; see ḵẖẉāja ḵẖiẓr, s.v. ḵẖẉāja.
more: n. m. The name of a prophet, commonly called khwājā-khizar . The god of wood and water; a guide. 1. Khizar mile jī! Khizar mile! Prov. Saying. I have found Khizar, I have found Khizir! (Eureka). 2. Dhobī chhoṛ saqqā kīyā, rahī Khizar ke ghāt . Prov. The dhobī she left and the bhīshti , wed at Khizar's ghāṭ she still remained. (Both toil in water, so she remained where she was). (Said of a distinction without a difference). khuājah-khizar . A saint skilled in divination, who is said to have discovered the water of life. Hence, a god of waters. Mahomedans ofter oblations to him of lamps, flowers, etc. Placed on little rafts launched on the river. It is in his honor that the feast of Beṛā , q. v. is held. Travellers by boat always invoke him on starting. ' Ai Khuājah Khizār beṛā pār!'
n. prop. A companion of Moses, Phineas: or, the prophet Elias.

def: s.m. = khoʼā or khoyā, q.v. s.m. Pounded brick, coarse brickdust (syn. surkhī); mortar, plaster; — milk inspissated by boiling, curds.
more: n. f. A cave; den; pit.

def: s.f. Nature, disposition, temper; habit, custom; way, manner
more: n. f. See H. بان 3. Laṛtī ho, jhagaṛtī ho har ek bāt pe, khānam, Khū kaisī burī ho gaī, vallāh, tumhārī! Wom. kho-bo, khū-bū , n. f. Ways; manners. kho paṛnā , v. a. To become a habit; grow upon one ( bān sīkhnā ). kho ḍālnā , v. a. See bān ḍālnā , 1, 2. kho-gar, kho-giriftah , adj. Accustomed; used to.

s. f. Habit, custom, disposition, nature. خوگر يا پذير يا گرفته ḵẖo-gar or -pazīr or -girifta, adj. Accustomed, habituated. خوڐالنا ḵẖo ḍālnā, To habituate, to adopt a habit, to introduce a custom, to accustom.
subs: v.n. A habit to be acquired; to become a habit
(-kī), To naturalize; to habituate, accustom, use (to), inure; to train; to exercise; to adopt a habit; to introduce a custom or practice
adj. Rendered well-behaved, or tractable, or mild; tamed; — accustomed, habituated.

def: s.m. Pounded brick, coarse brickdust (syn. surkhī); mortar, plaster; — milk inspissated by boiling, curds.
more: s. m. 1. Plaster. 2. Coarse brick dust. 3. Milk, inspissated by boiling.

def: part. & s.m. = khoyā, q.v. part. adj. & s.m. (f. khoʼī), Lost; destroyed; thrown away, &c.; — any thing or person that is lost; that which is thrown away; refuse, dregs.
s.m. A hog-sty.
s.f. = khoṅć, q.v. s.f. Scratch, rent (in cloth, &c.), rip, puncture, stab
subs: or khoṅć lagnā (-ko, or -meṅ), To receive a scratch, or rip, &c.; to be ripped, to be torn (by a nail, &c.).

def: s.m. = khoṅćā, q.v. s.m. Thrust; stab, prick; stuffing
subs: s.f. Thrusting, stuffing; — mutual tugging and pulling, mutual wrangling and quarrelling, a scrimmage.

def: s.m. = khoṅćaṛ, q.v. s.m. One who thrusts himself where he is not wanted; an obtrusive, officious fellow; a mean fellow.
n. 1. One who puts a spoke in other people's affairs. 2. A mean fellow.

ety: Zend khaodha, fr. khud; S. kuha
def: s.m. A helmet, steel head-piece.
ex: میں یہ کام خود کروں گا۔
I will do this work myself.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خْوَد (xwad)/خْوُد (xwud).

def: s.f. Digging; dig; — push, thrust, pass, stab
more: s. m. A push, a thrust, a pass. ¶ khūd, Refuse.
subs: s.f. Strict or close search; searching inquiry.

def: s.f. prop. khudāʼī, q.v.
s. (dakh.) Agriculture ( Harris ).
(caus. of کهودنا) v. a. To cause to dig.
v.t. prop. khudānā, q.v.
adj. Pitted; rough, uneven, &c. ( = khudrā); — s.m. A kind of pace (in a horse), a trot, an amble (see khudrānā).
more: s. m. Name of a certain pace (of a horse).

ety: S. कोटर; or khoḍa = khoda (see khodnā)+S. रः
def: s.m. A pit, cavity, hole, hollow (in a tree, &c.; cf. kholaṛ).

adj. = khudrā, q.v. s.f. = kshudrā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Small, little, minute, trifling, insignificant; indigent, poor; mean, low, vile, base, contemptible; niggardly, avaricious; — a mean person, &c.
subs: adj. & s.m.f. Of little understanding, simple, silly, foolish, ignorant; — a person of little understanding, or of mean intellect
s.m. Any lower animal, a brute beast; — kshudra-rog, s.m. A minor disease, a disease of little importance
s.m. (f. -iṇī), A person suffering from a minor disease.

def: adj. Sixteenth.
s.f.(?) (Dakh.) = khodnī, q.v. s.f. Digging, &c.; — searching; investigating; — instrument for digging; a spade; engraving-tool; carving-tool (cf. khudnī)
subs: To search for by digging; to dig for treasure; to search for, investigate; to find out.

def: s. m. Search, inquiry.
s.f. (dialec.) Digging, delving; culture (of land).

ety: khod˚, prob. = Prk. खोद(इ) or खोदे(इ) = S. क्षोदय(ति), caus. of rt. क्षुद्
def: v.t. To dig, delve, excavate; to undermine; to scoop, hollow; to engrave; to carve; — to search for; to inquire closely into, to investigate
more: v. a. S. खन् to dig. 1. To dig; delve; excavate; scoop. ' Chanḍī, ghar līpegī?' 'Nahīṅ, nigoṛe, khodūṅgī.' 'Chanḍī, ghar khodegī?' 'Nahīṅ, nigoṛe, līpūngī .' Wom. Prov. 'You shrew, will you plaster the floor?' 'No, you wretch! I'll dig it up.' 'You shrew, will you dig the floor?' 'No, you wretch! I'll plaster it.' 2. To engrave; carve. 3. To investigate ( tahqīqāt k .). khod-kar yā khod khod-ke pūchhnā , v. a. To question or investigate closely; cross-question; prove; fathom. khod-gar, chod-gar , n. m. Slang. A father.
v. a. To dig, delve, hollow, engrave, carve, undermine. 2. To search for.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To dig up; to scoop out, &c.
wety: Derived from Prakrit *𑀔𑁆ō𑀤𑁆𑀤𑁆 (*khōdd). Cognate with Punjabi ਖੋਦਣਾ (khodṇā), Gujarati ખોદવું (khodvũ), Marathi 𑘏𑘻𑘟𑘜𑘹𑘽 (khodṇẽ), Nepali खोदनु (khodanu), Bengali খোদা (khōda).

def: s.f. Digging, &c.; — searching; investigating; — instrument for digging; a spade; engraving-tool; carving-tool (cf. khudnī)
more: s. f. 1. Searching, investigation. 2. A spade. کهودني کرنا khodnī karnā, To search, inquire, investigate.
subs: To search for by digging; to dig for treasure; to search for, investigate; to find out.

def: v.t. prop. khudwānā, q.v.
adj. See s.v. ḵẖo. s.f. Nature, disposition, temper; habit, custom; way, manner

def: s.m. The stuffing of a saddle; a pad; a saddle-cloth; a pack-saddle
subs: s.f. Padding or stuffing of a saddle; rags, rubbish, trash.

def: adj. See s.v. ḵẖo. s.f. Nature, disposition, temper; habit, custom; way, manner
more: n. m. 1. The stuffing of a saddle; a pad; a packsaddle; a saddle on which the shafts of an Ikkā rest. 2. A cant term for a turban. Kahāṅ ko khogīr kasā? What have you got yourself up for? khogīr kī bhartī , n. 1. Rags; rubbish; sweepings. Phr. Leather and prunella; odds and ends; a man of straw; a good-for-nothing. 2. Bad poetry (all padding).
s. m. The stuffing of a saddle, a pad, a packsaddle. خوگير دوز ḵẖogīr-doz, s. m. A saddle-maker.
subs: v.n. A habit to be acquired; to become a habit
(-kī), To naturalize; to habituate, accustom, use (to), inure; to train; to exercise; to adopt a habit; to introduce a custom or practice
adj. Rendered well-behaved, or tractable, or mild; tamed; — accustomed, habituated.

def: s.m.f. = kho, 'a cave,' &c., q.v. s.f. Nature, disposition, temper; habit, custom; way, manner
more: s. m. f. A cavern, abyss, a pit.
subs: v.n. A habit to be acquired; to become a habit
(-kī), To naturalize; to habituate, accustom, use (to), inure; to train; to exercise; to adopt a habit; to introduce a custom or practice
adj. Rendered well-behaved, or tractable, or mild; tamed; — accustomed, habituated.

def: s.f. A hood-form covering of leaves, &c. to shade plantlets, &c.); a kind of hood (worn as a protection against rain); clothes folded up and put on the head (as a defence against rain, &c.).
more: n. f. 1. Husks; refuse. 2. The refuse of sugarcane, used as fuel. Sīs kāṭ-kar tan mathā, pinjar dīyā sukhāë, Apne pī ke kārne maiṅ pher jalūṅgī āë . Riddle. Ans. Khoī . Refuse of Sugarcane. My head cut off, my body crushed I gave to dry, All for my love in flames I'll burn again and die. 3. Swollen parched rice. 4. A kind of hood, worn as a protection from rain ( choṭ ).

def: s.f. Swollen parched grain (esp. rice; syn. khīl, lāwā).
s. f. 1. The dry part of sugarcane after expressing the juice. 2. Clothes folded up and put on the head as a defence against rain.
more: s. (dakh.) Swollen parched rice ( Qanoon-e-islam, index under Dhan). کهوٴي نکلنا khoʼī nikalnā, To perspire freely ( Harris ).
